hard.next_step: JRA 6/11: Checked in with Neal and Joanna to discuss next steps with their IT team.
hard.ais_why: On the April 2026 discovery, Neal Parkinson asked 'How would the AI work?' and probed how AI would capture raw material specs accurately and reduce manual entry. Joanna Garcia voiced interest in AI-driven strategic sourcing for complex chemical ingredients but is gated on cost and IT integration across Sage, Cisco, SAP. Mid-funnel curiosity, no agentic POC, no Merlin-specific pull, no champion commitment yet.

north_star_verdict.headline: One warm intro call on 8 Apr 2026 landed well, then the deal went dark for 89 days: no pricing delivered, no IT-alignment meeting held, no economic buyer engaged, and on 22 Jun the close date slid seven months from 24 Jul 2026 to 25 Feb 2027. With no rival in the field, the threat is a quiet no-decision as Actylis's internal IT revamp crowds out procurement automation. The move is owner-led re-engagement of Neal Parkinson to book the IT/ERP-integration conversation he said must happen first.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-06. Days to close 234 (close 2027-02-25). Created 2026-04-08, 89 days old, entire life in Qualified stage. Last two-way buyer engagement: 2026-04-08 intro call, 89 days ago. Last Salesforce activity 2026-05-07 (outbound summit invite, no reply), 60 days ago. Rep-initiated Next Step 2026-06-11 (no buyer reply documented), 25 days ago. Close date pushed OUT 2026-07-24 to 2027-02-25 on 2026-06-22 (a 7-month deceleration, not a slip toward close). Cold for 89 days > the 60-day Off Track threshold; forced Off Track regardless of the far-out date.
evidence: Last two-way buyer engagement was the 2026-04-08 intro call (89 days ago): Neal Parkinson and JoAnna Garcia raised cost, timeframe, AI mechanics for complex chemical-ingredient sourcing, and multi-ERP integration, but committed to no next step.
Outbound Agentic AI Summit invites sent 2026-05-07 to both Neal and JoAnna (LastActivityDate) drew no response, 60 days ago.
Rep-initiated check-in 2026-06-11 'Checked in with Neal and Joanna to discuss next steps with their IT team' shows a rep-side touch with no documented buyer reply (Next_Step__c).
Close date pushed out 7 months on 2026-06-22 (2026-07-24 to 2027-02-25 by Justin Ajmo), a deceleration signal consistent with Actylis deprioritizing procurement automation behind its IT revamp.

No economic buyer engaged: Neal Parkinson (Global Purchasing Director, Decision Maker) and JoAnna Garcia (Procurement Manager, Evaluator) are the only mapped contacts, both mid-level; the budget owner above them is unnamed and unengaged.

Neal gated advancement on IT on the 2026-04-08 call: 'we are also going through an IT revamp, so I have to link into those guys as well before we go too far with this' — no IT meeting held in 89 days.
No pricing, no timeline, no AI/Merlin demo, and no ROI case delivered against the buyer's explicit 8 Apr asks; engagement remains a generic intro with Showpad videos.
No third-party competitor named in any call or field; the real competitor is status quo / do-nothing amid a competing internal IT project.

metrics.narrative: The operational pain is clear but never quantified. On the 2026-04-08 intro call JoAnna Garcia described fully manual procurement ('there's no automation of our procurement, I review data, I enter the purchase orders, I create them and send them') and manual supplier management by 'emails or phone calls, or visits, pretty much weekly,' while Neal Parkinson wanted 'real time feedback with regards to spend.' Neither attached a number: no PO volume, no hours lost to manual entry, no error rate, no target savings. For a $125k six-module deal there is no baseline and no ROI case, so no dollar figure exists to justify spend to a budget owner. Quantifying the manual-effort and spend-visibility cost on a real Actylis use case is the ranked move.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-04-08 (JoAnna Garcia on manual PO entry; Neal Parkinson on real-time spend feedback)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. Neal Parkinson (Global Purchasing Director, Salesforce Decision Maker, PRIMARY) raised the commercial question himself on 2026-04-08 ('how much is it gonna cost me? Both initial purchase plus licenses, packages of licenses for how many employees'), which shows he owns the buying conversation for procurement but not that he controls the budget. He explicitly deferred to two external gates: cost approval and an IT revamp ('I have to link into those guys before we go too far'). No CFO, CPO, or VP-level budget owner is named in any Salesforce contact role, field, or call, and the prior record's CPO/CFO names carried no source and are dropped as unverified. Identifying and accessing the budget owner above Neal is a ranked move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Neal Parkinson = Decision Maker/PRIMARY; JoAnna Garcia = Evaluator)
Avoma intro call 2026-04-08 (Neal on cost and IT-revamp gating)

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer named the capability criteria that matter but not a formal scorecard. On 2026-04-08 Neal Parkinson prioritized supplier risk/performance management, contract management, e-sourcing, spend analysis with 'real time feedback,' AI that can 'go out and source, especially complex things' like 'complex chemical ingredients,' and above all a single platform that integrates their three ERPs: 'how does this link up to those 3 ERPs... I would like to have one single platform.' JoAnna Garcia added accuracy of raw-material specs ('we have different specs that have to be followed for our raw material ingredients... how AI will pick that up') and eliminating manual data entry. These are stated priorities, not a ranked or weighted evaluation, and no RFP/RFI, workshop, or ShoeFit round has run. Cost and implementation timeframe are the two decisive unknowns the buyer flagged.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-04-08 (Neal on single-platform multi-ERP integration and complex sourcing; JoAnna on spec accuracy and manual entry)

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is undefined. The call sequence is a single intro call on 2026-04-08 attended by Neal Parkinson and JoAnna Garcia (both procurement), with no one from IT, finance, or executive leadership joining at any point, and no follow-on meeting booked. Neal named two sequencing gates but no owner or timeline: an IT revamp he must 'link into' before proceeding, and a cost/budget approval. No decision date, no approval chain, and no stakeholders beyond the two procurement contacts have surfaced. The 7-month close-date push on 2026-06-22 suggests the process has been paused behind Actylis's internal IT project rather than progressed. Mapping who signs off and getting IT into the room is the process-defining move.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-04-08 (single-thread procurement-only; Neal on IT-revamp and cost gates)

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are visible. The deal is at Qualified with no pricing issued, no proposal, no MSA/Order Form/SOW activity, no procurement channel, and no InfoSec or legal engagement, which is normal this early. The only structural constraint surfaced is that Actylis is mid-IT-revamp and runs multiple ERPs (Sage X3 at Utah, Cisco and SAP at other acquired sites), which will make integration scoping and InfoSec review substantive when the deal advances. There is no contract-expiration forcing function since procurement is fully manual today (no incumbent tool to displace). Delivering pricing and securing the IT/ERP-integration conversation would create the first paper-process visibility.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-04-08 (Neal on multi-ERP landscape and IT revamp; JoAnna on Sage X3 live since Feb 2024)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is concrete and confirmed, though qualitative. JoAnna Garcia stated Actylis has 'no automation of our procurement' and manually reviews data and creates/sends every PO in Sage X3 (live only since Feb 2024), managing suppliers 'through emails or phone calls, or visits' weekly, and lacking spend visibility and error control ('how it's gonna save me the manual data entry... create more visibility, and then errors'). Neal Parkinson owns the strategic pain: fragmented procurement across acquired sites on three ERPs with no single platform and no real-time spend feedback, plus a need to source 'complex chemical ingredients' intelligently. The pain owners are the procurement team (JoAnna operationally, Neal strategically). What is missing is not the pain but its cost quantification and the urgency to fix it now versus after the IT revamp.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-04-08 (JoAnna on manual PO entry and no automation; Neal on multi-ERP fragmentation and complex sourcing)

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Neal Parkinson and JoAnna Garcia engaged well on the 2026-04-08 intro call and asked good operational questions, and Neal said 'I like pretty much everything I see there,' but both behaved as information-gathering evaluators, not advocates: no internal selling, no introduction to IT or a budget owner, no agenda-driving, and no inbound pull. Neither responded to the 2026-05-07 summit invites or the 2026-06-11 rep check-in, and 89 days have passed with no buyer-initiated contact. A champion at this stage would have convened the IT meeting Neal said was required or requested the pricing they asked for. Developing Neal into a coach by arming him with a quantified ROI case and a Merlin proof point he can sell internally is the prerequisite move before any commercial step.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-04-08 (evaluator behavior; Neal 'I like pretty much everything I see')
SF LastActivityDate 2026-05-07 and Next_Step__c 2026-06-11 (no buyer reply); SF OpportunityContactRole (no Champion role)

competition.narrative: There is no third-party vendor competition; the competitor is status quo / do-nothing. No rival (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer, Zip) was named on the 2026-04-08 call or in any Salesforce field, and the buyer's questions were exploratory ('how would the AI work?', 'how much is it gonna cost me?') rather than comparative. Actylis runs fully manual procurement today, so we are displacing spreadsheets, email, and ERP-native entry, not an installed suite. The active threat is inertia amplified by a competing internal IT revamp that Neal said must resolve first, evidenced by the 7-month close-date push. The wedge is Merlin AI for complex chemical-ingredient sourcing plus single-platform integration across Sage/Cisco/SAP; activation is a live Merlin demo on a real Actylis use case plus a quantified cost-of-manual-work case to make doing nothing expensive.
sources: Avoma intro call 2026-04-08 (no rival named; capability-not-comparison questions)

deal_movement.summary: One intro call on day one (2026-04-08), then 89 days of one-way rep touches and a decelerating close date. The 2026-06-22 push of the close from 24 Jul 2026 to 25 Feb 2027 is the loudest signal: the deal has been parked behind Actylis's internal IT revamp, not advanced.

items.change: Intro call held with Neal Parkinson and JoAnna Garcia; strong operational discovery, no committed next step
Agentic AI Summit invites sent to Neal and JoAnna, no response
Rep check-in on IT next steps logged, no buyer reply documented
Close date pushed out 7 months, 2026-07-24 to 2027-02-25

competitive_position.summary: No active third-party competitor in any source; the single strongest current threat is status quo / do-nothing, sharpened by Actylis's parallel internal IT revamp that Neal Parkinson said must resolve before procurement automation proceeds (2026-04-08), consistent with the 7-month close-date push on 2026-06-22. We win by making inaction costly: a Merlin AI proof point on complex chemical-ingredient sourcing plus a quantified manual-effort/spend-visibility ROI case.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Actylis wants a single AI-enabled platform that unifies procurement across three ERPs and automates their fully manual process; Zycus fits the stated needs on paper, but integration complexity and cost are unproven and gate the fit.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal, single-threaded on two mid-level procurement contacts and cold for 89 days; the win levers are multi-threading to power and IT, converting curiosity into a Merlin proof point, and quantifying the cost of doing nothing.
flags: Multi-thread beyond Neal and JoAnna: get the ERP/IT owners and the budget holder above Neal into the conversation, since he gated progress on both.
Convert AI curiosity to pull with a live Merlin demo on a real Actylis complex chemical-ingredient sourcing case, not more Showpad videos.
Build a quantified ROI case (manual PO hours, spend visibility, error reduction) so status-quo inertia during the IT revamp becomes the costly choice.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Neal Parkinson and JoAnna Garcia engaged as evaluators on the single 2026-04-08 call but neither advocates internally, introduced IT or a budget owner, or responded to the last two rep touches. Nobody is driving the deal, which is why it has been dark for 89 days. The path is to develop Neal into a coach with a quantified ROI case and a Merlin proof point he can carry to IT and the budget owner.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin/AI story was introduced conceptually on 2026-04-08 (guided buying, AP Smartdesk, proactive risk) and both contacts were curious, but no Merlin demo or agentic proof point has been put in front of the specific chemical-sourcing use case they asked about, so the AI narrative is under-positioned relative to their stated interest.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 Moderate matches the calls: Neal ('How would the AI work?') and JoAnna (AI capturing raw-material specs) showed genuine but operational, cost-gated curiosity, not vision-level pull. No agentic POC and no Merlin-specific ask yet; curiosity is real but not converted.

items.detail: No economic buyer or executive sponsor engaged; only two mid-level procurement contacts, budget owner unnamed.
Close date pushed 7 months (24 Jul 2026 to 25 Feb 2027) on 2026-06-22, signaling deprioritization behind an internal IT revamp.
Multi-ERP landscape (Sage X3, Cisco, SAP across acquired sites) makes integration scoping a real barrier, and IT has not been engaged.
Status-quo inertia: fully manual procurement is tolerated today and the buyer sees no forcing event, risking a quiet no-decision.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling to cold: one call 89 days ago, two unanswered rep touches, and a decelerating close date. No buyer-initiated activity in the record.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A single warm 8 Apr intro call that landed well but produced no next step; 89 days silent since, no pricing delivered, no economic buyer, and on 22 Jun the close date slid seven months to 25 Feb 2027. With no rival in the field, status-quo inertia behind an internal IT revamp is what is beating us.

win_position.text: This sits near the bottom of the range: after Neal Parkinson said 'I like pretty much everything I see' on the 8 Apr intro call, the deal went dark for 89 days with no pricing, no IT meeting, and no economic buyer engaged.
Neal pushed the close from 24 Jul 2026 to 25 Feb 2027 on 22 Jun, signaling Actylis's IT revamp has pushed procurement automation down its priority list.

Coverage is single-threaded on two mid-level procurement contacts, Neal Parkinson (Global Purchasing Director) and JoAnna Garcia (Procurement Manager), with no budget owner or IT stakeholder in the room.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer-initiated activity since the 8 Apr intro call; the 7 May summit invites and the 11 Jun rep check-in both went unanswered.
The 22 Jun close-date push to 25 Feb 2027 decelerated the deal rather than advancing it.

customer_commitment.text: Neal gated progress on an internal IT revamp ('I have to link into those guys before we go too far') and no IT meeting has been booked in 89 days.
The buyer asked for cost and timeframe on 8 Apr but has not chased either and has requested no proposal.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded with no economic buyer, no champion, and no competitor to force a decision, the primary risk is a quiet no-decision while Actylis finishes its IT project.
The multi-ERP landscape (Sage X3, Cisco, SAP across acquired sites) makes integration scoping a real barrier that IT has not yet engaged on.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer touch since 2026-04-08; close date pushed out 7 months on 2026-06-22.

customer_commitment.evidence: Buyer gated on IT and cost, ignored two rep touches, requested no proposal.

deal_risk.evidence: Single-thread, no EB, no champion, status-quo no-decision risk amid an internal IT revamp.

customer_preference.evidence: Neal said 'I like pretty much everything I see' on 2026-04-08 but was non-comparative, gated on cost and IT, and then went silent for 89 days with no Zycus-specific next step requested.

business_case.evidence: Clear qualitative pain (fully manual PO entry, no spend visibility, fragmented multi-ERP procurement) but zero quantification of savings, hours, or error rates.

critical_signals.text: The 8 Apr intro call with Neal and JoAnna went well and covered pain, AI, and multi-ERP integration, but produced no committed next step and 89 days of silence followed.
No rival is in play; Actylis runs fully manual procurement, so the real fight is against status-quo inertia while their internal IT revamp takes priority.
Neal asked for pricing and license packaging on 8 Apr but none has been delivered, and the budget owner above him is unknown.
Neal named IT/ERP owners across Sage, Cisco, and SAP as gatekeepers he must involve, but Zycus has never met them.
